meta.name: Mahogany Cake (Southern Chocolate Layer Cake)
meta.author: SSA Canonical Catalog
meta.description: A classic Southern-style mahogany cake: a deep chocolate, tender-but-stackable layer cake with a distinctive dark color and rich cocoa flavor (often associated with old-school community cookbooks). Built as a reliable layer-cake baseline with both all-purpose flour and fresh-milled flour guidance (choose one). Includes a traditional cooked chocolate frosting option (fudge/boiled style) and a simpler buttercream option.

equipment.label: Saucepan (for cocoa bloom and/or cooked frosting)

ingredients.label: Neutral oil (canola/avocado/grapeseed)
ingredients.notes: About 1/4 cup; helps keep cake moist even when chilled.

ingredients.label: Cocoa powder (natural preferred for classic color; Dutch ok)
ingredients.notes: About 3/4 cup. Natural cocoa gives a brighter chocolate note.

ingredients.label: Hot coffee or hot water (for cocoa bloom)
ingredients.notes: About 3/4 cup. Coffee deepens chocolate but doesn’t taste like coffee.

ingredients.label: All-purpose flour (Option A — use this OR the fresh-milled option)
Fresh-milled flour, fine-milled (Option B — use this OR the AP option)
ingredients.notes: Fresh-milled can absorb more and may include more bran; for a smoother crumb, sift out the largest bran. Batter may need 15–45 ml extra buttermilk.

ingredients.label: Extra buttermilk (only if needed for fresh-milled flour batter)
ingredients.notes: Add 15 ml at a time if batter is too thick with fresh-milled flour; up to ~45 ml total depending on flour.

ingredients.label: Apple cider vinegar (optional, classic mahogany tang)
ingredients.notes: About 1 tsp. Optional; boosts lift with baking soda and adds subtle tang.

ingredients.label: Frosting choice: cooked chocolate frosting OR chocolate buttercream
ingredients.notes: Choose one frosting method below; both ingredient sets are included.

steps.title: Prep pans and oven
steps.body: Heat oven to 350°F (175°C). Grease two 9-inch round pans, line bottoms with parchment, then lightly grease parchment and dust with flour (or use baking spray).

steps.title: Choose your flour option
steps.body: Use either the all-purpose flour OR the fresh-milled flour (not both). Fresh-milled flour may need extra buttermilk and benefits from a 5–10 minute batter rest after mixing.

steps.title: Bloom the cocoa for deep color
steps.body: In a bowl or saucepan, whisk cocoa powder into hot coffee/water until smooth and glossy. Let cool slightly (warm is fine). This deepens flavor and helps develop the mahogany tone.

steps.title: Whisk dry ingredients
steps.body: In a medium bowl, whisk chosen flour, baking soda, baking powder, and salt.

steps.title: Cream butter and sugar (structure for layers)
steps.body: Beat softened butter and sugar on medium-high until light and fluffy, 3–5 minutes. Scrape the bowl. Beat in oil until combined.

steps.title: Add eggs and vanilla
steps.body: Add eggs one at a time, beating until incorporated after each. Mix in vanilla. Scrape bowl.

steps.title: Alternate dry + buttermilk + cocoa
steps.body: On low speed, add dry ingredients in 3 additions, alternating with buttermilk in 2 additions, mixing just until combined (dry → buttermilk → dry → buttermilk → dry). Then mix in the bloomed cocoa mixture until uniform. If using vinegar, stir it in at the end.

steps.title: Adjust batter for fresh-milled flour
steps.body: If using fresh-milled flour and batter is very thick, add extra buttermilk 15 ml (1 tbsp) at a time until batter is smooth and pourable. Rest batter 5–10 minutes to hydrate, then gently fold once more.

steps.title: Divide and level
steps.body: Divide batter evenly between pans. Smooth tops and tap pans gently to release large bubbles.

steps.title: Bake
steps.body: Bake 28–36 minutes, or until tops spring back and a toothpick in the center comes out clean or with a few moist crumbs. Rotate pans halfway if needed.

steps.title: Cool completely
steps.body: Cool in pans 10 minutes, then turn out onto racks, peel parchment, and cool completely before frosting.

steps.title: Choose and make frosting
steps.body: Choose either the cooked chocolate frosting (traditional, fudgy) or the chocolate buttercream (simpler, faster). Make frosting while cakes cool.

steps.title: Cooked chocolate frosting option (traditional)
steps.body: In a saucepan, whisk sugar, cocoa, milk, butter, and a pinch of salt. Bring to a gentle boil over medium heat, whisking. Reduce to a steady simmer and cook 3–5 minutes until slightly thickened and glossy. Remove from heat, stir in vanilla, then cool 10–20 minutes until spreadable (it thickens as it cools).

steps.title: Chocolate buttercream option (simple)
steps.body: Beat butter until creamy. Add powdered sugar and cocoa in stages with a pinch of salt, beating on low then medium. Add vanilla and enough milk/cream to reach a smooth, spreadable consistency.

steps.title: Stack and frost
steps.body: Level layers if needed. Place first layer on a plate/board, add frosting, then top with second layer (top-side down for a flatter top). Apply a thin crumb coat, chill 15 minutes, then finish frosting.

notes: Mahogany cake character: deep cocoa color and a tender-but-layerable crumb; cocoa bloom + buttermilk + soda helps develop flavor and color.
Fresh-milled flour: sift out coarse bran for a smoother crumb; batter may need a little extra buttermilk and a short rest.
Cooked frosting thickens as it cools—spread while still slightly warm for easiest application.
If you want even darker color, use natural cocoa and hot coffee; Dutch-process cocoa can be darker but may taste smoother/less sharp.

storage.refrigerator: Covered: up to 5 days. Bring to room temp for best texture.
storage.freezer: Wrapped unfrosted layers: up to 2 months. Frosting: buttercream freezes well; cooked frosting is best fresh.
